item.title: Do you want to build a robot snowman?
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: On the latest episode of the Equity podcast, we recapped CEO Jensen Huang’s GTC keynote and debated what it means for Nvidia’s future.

item.title: Cursor admits its new coding model was built on top of Moonshot AI’s Kimi
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Building on top of a Chinese model feels particularly fraught right now.

item.title: Elon Musk unveils chip manufacturing plans for SpaceX and Tesla
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Elon Musk recently outlined ambitious plans for a chip-building collaboration Tesla and SpaceX — but he has a history of overpromising.

item.title: Delve accused of misleading customers with ‘fake compliance’
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: An anonymous Substack post accuses compliance startup Delve of “falsely” convincing “hundreds of customers they were compliant” with privacy and security regulations.

item.title: An exclusive tour of Amazon’s Trainium lab, the chip that’s won over Anthropic, OpenAI, even Apple
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Shortly after Amazon announced its $50 billion investment in OpenAI, AWS invited me on a private tour of the chip lab at the heart of the deal.

item.title: Are AI tokens the new signing bonus or just a cost of doing business?
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Maybe tokens really will become the fourth pillar of engineering compensation. But engineers might want to hold the line before embracing this as a straightforward win.

item.title: Publisher pulls horror novel ‘Shy Girl’ over AI concerns
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Hachette Book Group said it will not be publishing “Shy Girl” over concerns that artificial intelligence was used to generate the text.

item.title: Why Wall Street wasn’t won over by Nvidia’s big conference
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: Despite investor fears of an AI bubble, Nvidia's latest conference shows that most in the industry aren't concerned by that possibility.

item.title: New court filing reveals Pentagon told Anthropic the two sides were nearly aligned — a week after Trump declared the relationship kaput
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Anthropic submitted two sworn declarations to a California federal court late Friday afternoon, pushing back on the Pentagon's assertion that the AI company poses an "unacceptable risk to national security" and arguing that the government's case relies on technical misunderstandings and claims that were never actually raised during the months of negotiations.

item.title: Microsoft rolls back some of its Copilot AI bloat on Windows
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The company is reducing Copilot entry points on Windows, starting with Photos, Widgets, Notepad, and other apps.

item.title: What happened at Nvidia GTC: NemoClaw, Robot Olaf, and a $1 trillion bet
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: CEO Jensen Huang took the stage at Nvidia’s GTC conference this week in his signature leather jacket to deliver a two-and-a-half-hour keynote, projecting $1 trillion in AI chip sales through 2027, declaring that every company needs an “OpenClaw strategy,” and closing with a rambling Olaf robot that had to get its mic cut. The message was hard to miss: Nvidia […]

item.title: Nvidia has an OpenClaw strategy. Do you?
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo, Kirsten Korosec, Anthony Ha, Sean O'Kane
item.description: CEO Jensen Huang took the stage at Nvidia’s GTC conference this week in his signature leather jacket to deliver a two-and-a-half-hour keynote, projecting $1 trillion in AI chip sales through 2027, declaring that every company needs an “OpenClaw strategy,” and closing with a rambling Olaf robot that had to get its mic cut. The message was hard to miss: Nvidia […]

item.title: WordPress.com now lets AI agents write and publish posts, and more
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: New AI agents on WordPress.com could lower barriers to publishing while increasing machine-generated content across the web.

item.title: Trump’s AI framework targets state laws, shifts child safety burden to parents
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Trump’s AI framework pushes federal preemption of state laws, emphasizes innovation, and shifts responsibility for child safety toward parents while laying out lighter-touch rules for tech companies.

item.title: The best AI investment might be in energy tech
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Power has become one of the biggest bottlenecks in rolling out new AI data centers. That's creating an opening for investors.

item.title: These AI notetaking devices can help you record and transcribe your meetings
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: These physical notetakers transcribe audio and give users summaries and action items of meetings using AI. Some even offer live translation.

item.title: Jeff Bezos reportedly wants $100 billion to buy and transform old manufacturing firms with AI
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The Amazon magnate has a new project centered around acquiring industrial firms and revamping them with AI technology.

item.title: Online bot traffic will exceed human traffic by 2027, Cloudflare CEO says
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: AI bots may outnumber humans online by 2027, says Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince, as generative AI agents dramatically increase web traffic and infrastructure demands.

item.title: Meta rolls out new AI content enforcement systems while reducing reliance on third-party vendors
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Meta believes these AI systems can detect more violations with greater accuracy, better prevent scams, respond more quickly to real-world events, and reduce over-enforcement.

item.title: DoorDash launches a new ‘Tasks’ app that pays couriers to submit videos to train AI
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Delivery couriers will be able to earn money by completing activities like filming everyday tasks or recording themselves speaking in another language.
